questions.explanation: Underwriting is the process used to determine entry into the group. Underwriting includes assessing the risk to determine the exposure of the group and determining the premium to be charged based on the risk.

title: Principles of Insurance — Test 4
description: IC-01 Practice Test 4 — Covers core principles, risk management, underwriting, reinsurance, and insurance concepts.

questions.question: Under which of the following circumstances, a fact is said to be material to the assessment of risk?
options: A fact is said to be material if it does not affect the decision of the underwriter to accept or reject the risk, or to determine the rates, terms and conditions if the risk is accepted
A fact is said to be material if the third-party, responsible for the accident is not absolved of his or her financial responsibility
A fact is said to be material, if it affects the decision of the underwriter to accept or reject the risk or to determine the rates, terms and conditions if the risk is accepted
A fact is said to be material if it does not involve more than one insurance policies drawn up on the same subject-matter and the insured cannot recover his or her loss from all the insurers
A fact is said to be material, if involves more than one insurance policies drawn up on the same subject-matter and the insured cannot recover his or her loss from all the insurers
questions.correctAnswer: A fact is said to be material, if it affects the decision of the underwriter to accept or reject the risk or to determine the rates, terms and conditions if the risk is accepted
questions.explanation: According to the principle of utmost good faith, the proposer is obliged to declare all relevant facts that are material to the assessment of the risk, at the time of making the proposal. A fact is said to be material, if it affects the decision of the underwriter to accept or reject the risk or to determine rates, terms and conditions if the risk is accepted.

questions.question: Full form of MLM is __________.
questions.explanation: Multi-level Marketing (MLM) or network marketing, is individuals selling products to the public - often by word of mouth and direct sales.

questions.question: Identify the correct statement from the following, with regards to a Free-Look-Period?
options: Discontinuing an existing policy and taking a new one for free
Choosing between two plans, one of which has considerably less premium or commission than the other
A prospective customer is allowed to look freely at the policy document within seven days and read it in depth
If a policy holder is not satisfied with the product, he or she can opt to return it to the insurance company within 15 days
Making a full and adequate disclosure of all the facts to allow the customers to make informed decisions
questions.correctAnswer: If a policy holder is not satisfied with the product, he or she can opt to return it to the insurance company within 15 days
questions.explanation: IRDA has prescribed a free look period of 15 days for life, general insurance and health policies. As per this stipulation, if a policy holder is not satisfied with the product, he/she can opt to return the same to the insurance company within a period of 15 days from the date of receipt of the document.

questions.question: Which of the following is evidence of an insurance contract?
questions.explanation: The insurance policy is the evidence of the contract and states the terms and conditions of the contract, details of promises made by the insurer, obligations of the policyholder and conditions attached thereto.

questions.question: Which of the following options is INCORRECT with regards to perils in fire insurance policies?
options: Perils relating to war-risks are excluded
Exclusions of perils are mentioned separately under the relevant section
Fire Insurance policies are 'Named Peril Policies', where the perils are listed
Perils such as impact of vehicles, riots, typhoons, cyclones, strike cannot be included
Add-on cover which are additional perils offered by the insurers may be opted for separately
questions.correctAnswer: Perils such as impact of vehicles, riots, typhoons, cyclones, strike cannot be included
questions.explanation: Fire insurance policies provide cover for financial losses due to damage to property arising out of fire, explosions etc. Fire policies may also cover damage through impact of vehicles, riots, typhoons, cyclones, strike or other malicious acts etc.

questions.question: In most cases, there is __________, when customers make purchases especially when purchasing tangible goods.
questions.explanation: When customers make purchases, especially when purchasing tangible goods, there is, in most cases an immediate sense of gratification or satisfaction from that purchase.

questions.question: Rahul single-handedly manages various departments and handles all the day to day activities of Rosewood Ltd. He is the ________ of Rosewood.
questions.explanation: A company has insurable interest in the life of certain important people whose absence can affect the company adversely. These people are key people and the company can take Keyman Insurance on the lives of these people.

questions.question: Identify from the following, the statement which is correct with respect to Valued-Policies.
options: Valued Policies are also known as Floater-Policies
Valued-Policies are also known as First-Loss Policies
Valued Policies are also known as Full-Value Policies
Valued Policies are also known as Declaration-Policies
Valued Policies are also known as Admitted-Value Polices
questions.correctAnswer: Valued Policies are also known as Admitted-Value Polices
questions.explanation: Valued policies are also called agreed value or admitted value policies, where the insurer agrees to pay a certain amount in the event of a total loss without taking into account allowances for depreciation or appreciation. This is not relevant for partial losses.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Contract?
options: Ram agrees with Shyam to kill Vishal for which Shyam agrees to pay Ram Rs. 10 Lakhs.
Renjit agrees to accompany Roshan for the weekend picnic.
Sudhir agrees to indemnify the loss in transit of smuggled goods of Shankar worth Rs. 10 Crores.
Rekha agrees to sell her house to Reshma for a consideration of Rs. 2 Crores.
questions.correctAnswer: Rekha agrees to sell her house to Reshma for a consideration of Rs. 2 Crores.
questions.explanation: Option 4 is considered as a contract as it is legally enforceable. All other options are not legally enforceable.

questions.question:
From the following data, calculate the Frequency of road accidents:
BRANCH | NUMBER OF THEFTS | NUMBER OF ACCIDENTS | NUMBER OF VEHICLES COVERED
A | 2 | 11 | 150
B | 3 | 19 | 160
C | 82 | 766 | 3600
D | 13 | 704 | 6090
questions.explanation: Total number of accidents = 11 + 19 + 766 + 704 = 1,500. Total number of vehicles covered = 150 + 160 + 3,600 + 6,090 = 10,000. Frequency = (Total accidents / Total vehicles) × 100 = (1,500 / 10,000) × 100 = 15%.

questions.question: When the Underwriter drafts the terms for the Policy for a risk underwritten, which function does he perform?
questions.explanation: Drafting the terms of cover is part of the Acceptance process.

questions.question: Which of the following Acts deals with Directors' Liability?
questions.explanation: The Companies Act, 2013 has significant provisions on liability of the Directors which are important for coverage under Directors and Officers Liability Insurance.

questions.question: What is Commoditisation?
options: Lack of distinction between different competing products
Pricing very competitively even by incurring losses
Pricing very high by creating a brand value
questions.correctAnswer: Lack of distinction between different competing products
questions.explanation: Commoditisation is lack of distinction between different competing products because Commoditisation treats every product as a commodity, and obscures distinctive feature of each product.

questions.question: A priori means __________.
options: Knowledge considered to be true without being based on previous experience
questions.correctAnswer: Knowledge considered to be true without being based on previous experience
questions.explanation: Knowledge considered to be true without being based on previous experience is known as a priori. You need not conduct an experiment to calculate the probability of getting a head in a toss of a coin. You know it a priori.

questions.question: Which of the following does not have an Insurable Interest?
options: Bailee's responsibility for goods entrusted to him.
Proposer's interest in the life of his friend.
Contractor on the building being constructed by him.
questions.correctAnswer: Proposer's interest in the life of his friend.
questions.explanation: While all the other three circumstances entail an Insurable Interest, no person is deemed to have an Insurable Interest in the life of a friend.

questions.question: Raju had a Health Insurance Policy for Rs.3,00,000. His Policy had a Co Pay of 20%. He was admitted to a Hospital for an Illness, and spent Rs.60,000. How much would he get from his Insurer?
questions.explanation: On the amount of loss of Rs.60,000, a 20% Co Pay is to be deducted which is Rs.12,000 (20% of 60,000). Hence (60,000 - 12,000) i.e. only Rs.48,000 is payable.

questions.question: A risk with a Sum Insured of Rs. 600 Crores is ceded to a Reinsurer under a Five Line Surplus Treaty. The Insurer's Retention is Rs. 100 Crores. If the loss is for Rs. 60 Crores, how much would the Reinsurer pay?
questions.explanation: Since the loss amount (Rs. 60 Crores) is less than the Insurer's Retention (Rs. 100 Crores), the Reinsurer would not pay anything in this scenario.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a Non-Proportional Reinsurance?
questions.explanation: Surplus is a Proportional Reinsurance arrangement.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: Probability of future losses is better estimated if the number of records of past losses is higher
The longer the period over which the data is Studied, the more effective would be the estimated probability of future events
Losses from emerging risks cannot be accurately predicted
The estimated probability will be effective even if the number of risks underwritten is low
questions.correctAnswer: The estimated probability will be effective even if the number of risks underwritten is low
questions.explanation: Even if the exercise of estimating future loss probabilities is properly carried out, with a large volume of records, over a long period of time, if the number of risks underwritten, based on such estimates, is less, the difference between actual outcome and expected outcome could be significant. The Law will work only when sufficiently large number of risks is underwritten.

questions.question: What is meant by Concurrent causes?
options: In All Risk Policies, where the cause could not be identified.
Two or more Perils occurring at the same time
Caused by Electric Short circuit and resulting in Fire.
Different Causes occurring independent of each other.
questions.correctAnswer: Two or more Perils occurring at the same time
questions.explanation: Some times two causes could be happening together, not necessarily one after the other, but simultaneously. Such simultaneous causes are called concurrent causes.

questions.question: If the Direct Claims Paid for the Current Year is Rs.12000 Crores, the Reserve for Outstanding Claims at the end of the Current Year is Rs.18000 Crores, and the Reserve for Outstanding Claims at the end of the Previous Year is Rs.16000 Crores, what is the Gross Incurred Claims for the Current Year?
questions.explanation: Gross Incurred Claims for the Current Year = Direct Claims Paid + Reserve for Outstanding Claims at the end of the Current Year - Reserve for Outstanding Claims at the end of the Previous Year = 12000 + 18000 - 16000 = Rs.14000 Crores.

questions.question: Proper Underwriting enables __________.
questions.explanation: A proper Underwriting would mean that the pool of the risks underwritten would be very similar to the pool of risks the Actuary had based her workings on. Proper Underwriting thus enables better loss prediction.

questions.question: The Statement of Assets and Liabilities of a Company as at the end of the financial year is called:
questions.explanation: The Statement of Assets and Liabilities of a Company as at the end of the financial year is called Balance Sheet.

questions.question: Identify the statement that is TRUE. Insurers agree to__________.
questions.explanation: Insurance is a contract that compensates the financial loss suffered by the Insured caused by an insured peril. The Insurers pay more than the premium received, because they collect premium money from various persons who are exposed to the same kind of risk, but not all people facing the same risk suffer losses.

questions.question: It is not feasible for the insurer to inspect each and every risk proposed for insurance as __________.
options: the insurer's information system is not designed for risk inspection
the insurer does not have knowledge of the risk
the policy carries the condition of Utmost Good Faith
the insurer is not interested in knowing about the risk
the number of risks proposed for insurance are many and it would not be feasible
questions.correctAnswer: the number of risks proposed for insurance are many and it would not be feasible
questions.explanation: An insurer receives hundreds of proposals from the public to cover their risks. It is also not feasible for the insurer to inspect each and every risk before accepting it for insurance. The customer who makes the proposal is expected to have intimate knowledge on the features of the risk proposed to be insured. The insurer, therefore, trusts the insured customer to disclose all facts material to the risk being proposed for insurance.

questions.question: Why is mysterious disappearance not covered?
options: Since reason of loss cannot be ascertained
Since it takes place very often
Since the amount of loss cannot be ascertained
questions.correctAnswer: Since reason of loss cannot be ascertained
questions.explanation: Some of the insurance policies cover only certain named Perils. Some policies cover All Risks, but even such All Risks policies exclude losses caused by a number of Perils. Hence, in order to determine admissibility, the insurer should know the cause of loss. That is why losses like mysterious disappearances are not covered.

questions.question: The insurer __________ in Liability Claims.
options: pays the insured only when he is held not liable
pays the insured after he fulfills the court order
pays the insured even though he does not fulfill the court order
deposits the money with the victim
makes payment only if the insured is not at fault
questions.correctAnswer: pays the insured after he fulfills the court order
questions.explanation: In liability insurance, the insurer typically indemnifies (compensates) the insured only after the insured has satisfied the court order or paid the claim to the third party (the victim). This ensures that the insured fulfils their legal obligations, and the insurer then reimburses the insured for the loss, as per the terms of the policy.

questions.question: Explain the relation between Burning cost and Premium.
options: Burning cost minus other cost elements
Burning cost loaded with other cost elements
questions.correctAnswer: Burning cost loaded with other cost elements
questions.explanation: Burning cost represents the historical claims experience for a particular risk and is used to estimate the premium required to cover expected future losses. In premium calculation, burning cost serves as the foundation, but additional cost elements such as operating expenses, commissions, profit margin, and risk adjustments are added to determine the final premium charged to policyholders.

questions.question: Reserve for IBNR is created when claim has occurred __________.
options: and has been reported in current year but not paid
and has been reported and also paid in the current year
in current year but has not been reported
and has been reported in previous year but paid in current year
and has been reported and paid in the previous year
questions.correctAnswer: in current year but has not been reported
questions.explanation: IBNR stands for 'Incurred But Not Reported.' It refers to claims that have already occurred (the insured event has taken place) by the end of an accounting period, but the insurance company has not yet received a claim report for them. This reserve ensures that insurers are financially prepared to handle such unknown liabilities.

questions.question: Choose the risks that a Risk manager should identify.
questions.explanation: Risk Management is initially concerned with identifying risks. An enterprise faces various risks. Some of them are from Pure Risks, some from Speculative Risks. It is the duty of Risk Management to identify all risks the enterprise is exposed to.

questions.question: Identify the statement that is NOT TRUE.
options: Insurers provide compensation for sentimental losses
Insurers agree to make good losses
Insurers provide compensation for financial losses
Insurers make payment only if losses arise out of an insured peril
Insurers make payment only if the insured has suffered a loss
questions.correctAnswer: Insurers provide compensation for sentimental losses
questions.explanation: Insurance is a loss sharing mechanism. Insurance is a contract that compensates the financial loss suffered by the Insured caused by an insured peril. Losses caused by uninsured or excluded Perils are not covered by insurance. Insurers do not compensate sentimental losses.

questions.question: Identify the unique feature of Proportional Treaties.
options: Just the premium is shared proportionately
There is equal sharing of risk
There is proportionate sharing of premium
There is equal sharing of loss
There is proportionate sharing of risk, premium and loss
questions.correctAnswer: There is proportionate sharing of risk, premium and loss
questions.explanation: The most striking feature of Proportional Treaties is that risks, premium and losses are shared between the Insurer and Reinsurer in some proportion. Non-Proportional Treaties, in contrast, do not share risks, premium and losses in any proportion.

questions.question: Hazard study is required to __________.
questions.explanation: A hazard study is typically conducted to identify and understand hazards in a given environment, process, or system. This helps in assessing potential risks and preventing accidents or losses. Hazard studies are crucial for safety management and risk mitigation strategies.

questions.question: Calculate the premium, when the burning cost is given as 1000, fixed cost is 200 and the other cost is 30%.
questions.explanation: Premium = (Burning Cost + Fixed Costs) / (1 - Other Costs) = (1000 + 200) / (1 - 0.30) = 1200 / 0.70 = Rs. 1714.29.

questions.question: Which of the following is considered to be the best measure for efficacy of underwriting?
questions.explanation: The efficacy of the Underwriting System should be periodically evaluated. Efficacy of Underwriting depends on how closely the accepted pool of risks matches the expected pool of risks. This is best measured by the loss ratios for the pool.

questions.question:
Identify the statement that is CORRECT.
Statement a: All perils are covered by some policies without exception
Statement b: All perils are not covered by All Risk Policies
options: a is correct in case b is correct
questions.explanation: In the insurance world, there is no Policy which covers all kinds of Perils. Even coverage under All Risks Policy does not cover all Perils. Therefore, in any Insurance, there are some Perils which are covered, there are some Perils which are not covered, and some Perils which are specifically excluded.

questions.question: Identify the statement that is TRUE
options: All speculative risks could lead to a loss
Pure risks could lead to profit or loss
Speculative risks could lead to profit or loss
All pure risks could lead to profit
All pure risks are speculative risks
questions.correctAnswer: Speculative risks could lead to profit or loss
questions.explanation: Risks could be grouped into Pure Risks and Speculative Risks. Speculative Risks could result in three possible outcomes: Profit, Loss and No Profit No Loss. Pure Risks are those that cause a loss, with no possibility of a gain.

questions.question: __________ premium is taken into account to calculate the profit of the company.
questions.explanation: Once the Net Earned Premium, Net Incurred Claims, Net Commission, Operating Expenses and Investment Income are calculated, Net Profit of the Company is worked out.

questions.question: If the insurer sells the salvage before settling the loss and receives the money for the sale, what will be the consequence?
options: The full amount of the loss is paid by the insurer and he also makes payment for salvage proceeds to the insured
The full amount of the loss is paid by the insurer without deducting for salvage
Payment for the claim is made by the insurer even though the claim is not admissible
Salvage proceeds are deducted by the insurer and balance amount is settled with the insured
The claim is denied by the insurer even if it is admissible
questions.correctAnswer: Salvage proceeds are deducted by the insurer and balance amount is settled with the insured
questions.explanation: In insurance claims involving property damage, if the insurer sells the salvage before settling the claim, they recover some money from it. To avoid overcompensation to the insured (which violates the principle of indemnity), the salvage value is deducted from the total claim amount. The insurer then pays only the net loss to the insured.

questions.question: __________ is not considered to be an example of Avoidance.
options: Not building the factory in an area which is earthquake prone
Not building the factory near a river
questions.explanation: Certain losses could be prevented by adopting safety measures like wearing helmets which is an example of Risk Mitigation, not Risk Avoidance. Not storing or manufacturing hazardous goods, not building in earthquake-prone areas, and not building near a river are all examples of Risk Avoidance.

questions.question:
Identify which of these two statements is TRUE.
A) If there is no deviation from the expected outcome, there is no risk
B) If there is no expectation, there is no risk
questions.explanation: Risk is the possibility of a deviation from an expected outcome. There is no risk that the sun will not rise in the east tomorrow — it unfailingly rises in the east, so there is no deviation and no risk. But not all things are as sure as the sunrise. If there is no expectation, there can be no risk.

questions.question: Salvage means __________.
questions.explanation: In a property loss, some amount could be saved from the damage. This amount depends on the nature of property insured, and the cause and extent of loss. In most cases, some value is recoverable as scrap. The value that could be recovered from the damaged property is called salvage. When a claim is settled, the salvage value is reduced from the amount payable.

questions.question: The underwriter should study __________ while analysing a risk.
options: perils covered and hazards involved therein
questions.correctAnswer: perils covered and hazards involved therein
questions.explanation: The task that the Insurer should undertake before deciding on whether to accept a risk or to decline a risk, is to first evaluate the risk. Evaluation begins with collection of information on the risk and its features. The information collected would help the Insurer understand the nature of risk, the context of its exposure, the Perils to be covered, and the hazards to which the risk is exposed.

questions.question: A balanced book involves __________.
questions.explanation: While every Insurer would like to write more and more of the same type of risk, concentration of business in one line, one region, one channel, one group of clients could seriously impair the Company's performance. A balanced book is one with diversified business from various geographies, clients, channels and products.

questions.question: Uberrimae Fidei is a __________ phrase.
questions.explanation: Insurance contracts are contracts of utmost good faith. The parties to the contract have a duty to disclose all material facts even without being asked. This is called the Duty of Disclosure. Uberrimae fidei is a Latin phrase, meaning Utmost Good Faith.

questions.question: What is meant by Loss development factor?
options: Development of aggregate liability during claims cycle
Study of underwriting to lower the loss
Development of profits during the period of loss
Hazard study to see how losses occur
questions.correctAnswer: Development of aggregate liability during claims cycle
questions.explanation: Determining the rates for different products is a very complex exercise. One of the factors to be considered is the Loss Development factor — the manner of development of aggregate liability over the claim maturity cycle.

questions.question: Identify the incorrect statement.
options: Profit is not included in price
Number of losses per number of exposure units constitutes frequency
Units of risk constitute exposure unit
Severity means average of the losses
Frequency multiplied by severity equals to burning cost
questions.correctAnswer: Profit is not included in price
questions.explanation: In insurance, price (premium) includes: Burning cost (expected claims), Expenses, Profit margin, and Contingencies. So, profit is indeed included in the insurance price to ensure the insurer's financial sustainability. Therefore, the incorrect statement is 'Profit is not included in price'.

questions.question: Identify the peril not applicable to a property.
questions.explanation: A peril refers to a specific cause of loss or damage to property, such as: Flood, Earthquake, Fire, Storm. All of the above are physical perils that can directly damage property. Liability is not a peril, but a type of insurance coverage that protects against legal responsibility for injury or damage to others.

questions.question: In India, the insurer has to be a Corporation or a Company incorporated under the ________.
questions.explanation: In India, insurers are required to be incorporated as a company under the Companies Act or be established as a corporation under a specific Act of Parliament.

questions.question: Which Indian act lays down the conditions of a valid contract and the when it becomes voidable, how it can be revoked etc.?
questions.explanation: The Indian Contract Act lays down the conditions of a valid contract, when it becomes voidable, and how it can be revoked. It forms the legal basis for all contracts in India, including insurance contracts.
